message:
test(readme): functional coverage for SqlQueryResult + SqlIndexedChunkIterator

Self-review of fd90.13 (SQL schema/iterator return-type promotions)
caught 2 types whose surface was named in the prelude but not driven
end-to-end. The other 7 fd90.13 types are functionally exercised by
SqlInspector method coverage in fd90.9-12; these two were the holdouts.

Extends readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite with:

- SqlQueryResult: call conn.query("SELECT id, name FROM parent ...")
  and assert .columns + .rows shape after seeding two parent rows
- SqlIndexedChunkIterator: read_sql_chunks_with_index_col with the
  named 'id' column promoted to the row index. Verifies the iterator
  yields a chunk with len==2 and the index_col is removed from data.

Also promotes read_sql_chunks_with_index_col to the prelude — it's the
basic chunks+index_col producer that pairs with SqlIndexedChunkIterator
(the other ~17 SQL index_col variants remain at crate root as
power-user surface).

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.20.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.18","title":"test(readme): functional coverage for fd90.15 helpers (index_to_frame/series, AggFunc, GroupByOptions)","description":"Self-review of fd90.15 (commit 37b4eb5) caught a gap parallel to fd90.17: the bead promoted 6 helpers to the prelude with a compile-only guard (fd90_015_misc_helpers_via_prelude). Nothing exercises them functionally end-to-end.

Plan: add readme_index_helpers_round_trip integration test reachable via prelude alone:
- index_to_frame(index, Some(name)) → DataFrame with the correct column
- index_to_frame(index, None) → DataFrame with default-named column
- index_to_series(index, Some(name)) → Series with correct labels
- AggFunc enum variant access via match (Sum, Mean, etc.)
- GroupByOptions / GroupByExecutionOptions Default + field access","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:13:25.145057476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:15:35.265938228Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:15:35.263925756Z","close_reason":"completed: functional integration test for fd90.15 helpers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.18","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:13:25.145057476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.19","title":"test(readme): functional coverage for fd90.16 helpers (DateRangeError/TimedeltaRangeError, cast_scalar_owned, read_csv_with_index_cols)","description":"Self-review of fd90.16 (commit c70c018) caught the same gap pattern as fd90.17/fd90.18: the bead promoted 5 paired helpers to the prelude with a compile-only guard. Nothing exercises them functionally.

Plan: add readme_paired_helpers_round_trip:
- Trigger TimedeltaRangeError::InsufficientParams via timedelta_range(None, None, None, ...)
- Trigger DateRangeError via insufficient params
- cast_scalar_owned: move-input cast vs cast_scalar borrow path on the same value
- read_csv_with_index_cols: parse a CSV and promote a column to the index","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:16:37.314206548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:18:22.868749036Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T12:18:22.866707149Z","close_reason":"completed: functional integration test for fd90.16 helpers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.19","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:16:37.314206548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.20","title":"test(readme): functional coverage for SqlQueryResult + SqlIndexedChunkIterator","description":"Self-review of fd90.13 found 2 SQL types whose surface is in prelude but not functionally exercised end-to-end (the other 7 fd90.13 types are produced by SqlInspector methods which are tested in fd90.9-12):

- SqlQueryResult — return type of SqlConnection::query(sql, params)
- SqlIndexedChunkIterator — return type of read_sql_chunks_with_index_col / etc.

Plan: extend readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite to:
- Call conn.query(...) directly and inspect SqlQueryResult.columns + .rows
- Call read_sql_chunks_with_index_col (or simplest variant) and verify SqlIndexedChunkIterator yields expected chunks","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:19:27.247170864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T12:20:01.097182927Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.20","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T12:19:27.247170864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","title":"fp-io SQL chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine pandas-style chunksize with index_col promotion. Cover read_sql/read_sql_query/read_sql_table chunk paths by wrapping SqlChunkIterator, preserving per-chunk RangeIndex when index_col is None and promoting/removing the named column when set.
